title: useReactFunctionComponentDefinition
description: Learn more about useReactFunctionComponentDefinition

import { Tabs, TabItem } from '@astrojs/starlight/components';

<Tabs>
<TabItem label="JSX and TSX" icon="seti:javascript">
:::caution
This rule is part of the [nursery](/linter/#nursery) group. This means that it is experimental and the behavior can change at any time.
:::
## Summary
- Rule available since: `v2.5.0`
- Diagnostic Category: [`lint/nursery/useReactFunctionComponentDefinition`](/reference/diagnostics#diagnostic-category)
- This rule has an [**unsafe**](/linter/#unsafe-fixes) fix.
- The default severity of this rule is [**information**](/reference/diagnostics#information).
- This rule belongs to the following domains:
  - [`react`](/linter/domains#react)
- Sources: 
  - Inspired from [`react/function-component-definition`](https://github.com/jsx-eslint/eslint-plugin-react/blob/master/docs/rules/function-component-definition.md)

## How to configure
```json title="biome.json"
{
	"linter": {
		"rules": {
			"nursery": {
				"useReactFunctionComponentDefinition": "error"
			}
		}
	}
}

```
## Description
Enforce a specific function type for React function components.

This rule keeps function component definitions consistent. By default, named
components must be written as function declarations.

## Examples

### Invalid

```jsx
const MyComponent = (props) => {
  return <div>{props.name}</div>;
};
```

<pre class="language-text"><code class="language-text">code-block.jsx:1:7 <a href="https://biomejs.dev/linter/rules/use-react-function-component-definition">lint/nursery/useReactFunctionComponentDefinition</a> <span style="color: #000; to allow an arrow function.</span><br />  <br />  <strong><span style="color: lightgreen;">ℹ</span></strong> <span style="color: lightgreen;">This rule belongs to the nursery group, which means it is not yet stable and may change in the future. style="color: MediumSeaGreen;">+</span> <span style="color: MediumSeaGreen;">&#125;</span><br />    <strong>4</strong> <strong>4</strong><strong> │ </strong>  <br />  <br /></code></pre>

### Valid

```jsx
function MyComponent(props) {
  return <div>{props.name}</div>;
}
```

## Options

### `namedComponents`

The function style to enforce for named React components.
Accepted values are:

- `"functionDeclaration"` (default): Enforce function declarations.
- `"functionExpression"`: Enforce function expressions assigned to component bindings.
- `"arrowFunction"`: Enforce arrow functions assigned to component bindings.

#### `"functionDeclaration"`

```json title='biome.json'
{
	"linter": {
		"rules": {
			"nursery": {
				"useReactFunctionComponentDefinition": {
					"level": "on",
					"options": {
						"namedComponents": "functionDeclaration"
					}
				}
			}
		}
	}
}

```

##### Invalid

```jsx
const MyComponent = (props) => {
  return <div>{props.name}</div>;
};
```

```jsx
function MyComponent(props) {

```

#### `"functionExpression"`

```json title='biome.json'
{
	"linter": {
		"rules": {
			"nursery": {
				"useReactFunctionComponentDefinition": {
					"level": "on",
					"options": {
						"namedComponents": "functionExpression"
					}
				}
			}
		}
	}
}

```

##### Invalid

```jsx
function MyComponent(props) {
  return <div>{props.name}</div>;
}
```

MediumSeaGreen;">o</span><span style="color: MediumSeaGreen;">n</span><span style="color: MediumSeaGreen;">s</span><span style="color: MediumSeaGreen;">t</span><span style="color: style="color: MediumSeaGreen;">o</span><span style="color: MediumSeaGreen;">m</span><span style="color: MediumSeaGreen;">p</span><span style="color: MediumSeaGreen;">o</span><span style="color: MediumSeaGreen;"><strong>c</strong></span><span style="color: MediumSeaGreen;"><strong>t</strong></span><span style="color: MediumSeaGreen;"><strong>i</strong></span><span style="color: 0.8;"><strong>·</strong></span></span><span style="color: MediumSeaGreen;">(</span><span style="color: MediumSeaGreen;">p</span><span style="color: MediumSeaGreen;">r</span><span style="color: MediumSeaGreen;">o</span><span style="color: MediumSeaGreen;">p</span><span style="color: MediumSeaGreen;">s</span><span style="color: MediumSeaGreen;">)</span><span style="color: MediumSeaGreen;"><span style="opacity: 0.8;">·</span></span><span style="color: MediumSeaGreen;">&#123;</span><br />    <strong>2</strong> <strong>2</strong><strong> │ </strong>    return &lt;div&gt;&#123;props.name&#125;&lt;/div&gt;;<br />    <strong>3</strong> <strong>3</strong><strong> │ </strong>  &#125;;<br />  <br /></code></pre>

##### Valid

```jsx
const MyComponent = function (props) {
  return <div>{props.name}</div>;
};
```

#### `"arrowFunction"`

```json title='biome.json'
{
	"linter": {
		"rules": {
			"nursery": {
				"useReactFunctionComponentDefinition": {
					"level": "on",
					"options": {
						"namedComponents": "arrowFunction"
					}
				}
			}
		}
	}
}

```

##### Invalid

```jsx
function MyComponent(props) {
  return <div>{props.name}</div>;
}
```

<pre class="language-text"><code class="language-text">code-block.jsx:1:10 <a href="https://biomejs.dev/linter/rules/use-react-function-component-definition">lint/nursery/useReactFunctionComponentDefinition</a> <span style="color: #000; background-color: #ddd;"> FIXABLE </span> ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━<br /><br />  <strong><span style="color: lightgreen;">ℹ</span></strong> <span style="color: lightgreen;">The React component MyComponent is defined as a function declaration.</span><br />  <br />  <strong><span style="color: Tomato;">&gt;</span></strong> <strong>1 │ </strong>function MyComponent(props) &#123;<br />   <strong>   │ </strong>         <strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><br />    <strong>2 │ </strong>  return &lt;div&gt;&#123;props.name&#125;&lt;/div&gt;;<br />    <strong>3 │ </strong>&#125;<br />  <br />  <strong><span style="color: lightgreen;">ℹ</span></strong> <span style="color: lightgreen;">Mixing component definition styles makes component declarations harder to scan.</span><br />  <br />  <strong><span style="color: lightgreen;">ℹ</span></strong> <span style="color: lightgreen;">Rewrite this component as an arrow function or configure &#96;namedComponents&#96; to allow a function declaration.</span><br />  <br />  <strong><span style="color: lightgreen;">ℹ</span></strong> <span style="color: lightgreen;">This rule belongs to the nursery group, which means it is not yet stable and may change in the future. ```jsx
const MyComponent = function (props) {

##### Valid

```jsx
const MyComponent = (props) => {
  return <div>{props.name}</div>;
};
```
